element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game via fortune-telling game event
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic introduced narratively before combat application
Available alongside Personas but secondary to them
Unlocks tarot cards and resource generation through emotional manipulation
Enables Persona fusion and customization progression
Igor's Persona fusion system; accessed from predetermined locations
Affects enemy demon activity patterns and moods; persistent environmental pressure
Generates consistent combat opportunities and resources

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to hometown (overhead view safe space)
typical_tension_triggers: Lunar phase shifts affecting enemy behavior
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based grid combat with negotiation subgame creates alternating resource-gathering and high-stakes interaction phases; lunar cycle provides ambient tension layer independent of player actions

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: First-person dungeon navigation signals mandatory progression; overhead hometown view signals safe social/story areas; angled third-person view signals optional exploration and encounter zones

difficulty_curve.early_game: Persona summoning introduces core mechanic; basic melee available as safety net; demon negotiation presents resource puzzle rather than combat pressure
notable_spikes: Lunar phase transitions alter demon behavior unpredictably
Story-mandatory battles force specific party configurations

notable_level_patterns.description: Combat encounters interrupt for emotional manipulation mini-game; four discrete emotional states (anger, fear, joy, interest) trigger after three successful provocations, yielding either attack, flight, item drop, or tarot card
Gameplay uses three distinct camera modes: overhead (safe hometown), angled third-person (exploration), and first-person (dungeons/buildings); shift signals environmental context and danger level
Persistent moon phase display determines NPC moods and demon activity patterns; creates ambient difficulty variance independent of player skill, forcing adaptation across multiple play sessions
